findings.impact: The unchanged export contract says nightly automation shells out without flags and expects the default output to be machine-readable JSON with a top-level version field. After this change, a no-flag invocation emits markdown instead, which will break those existing consumers.
findings.test_gap: The CI snapshot shows the json contract suite is skipped in this stack, so there is no runnable coverage here for the historical no-flag JSON contract.
findings.title: Changing the default output to markdown breaks no-flag automation callers

findings.impact: The CLI still treats --output json as a structured payload and passes the renderer result through json.dumps. Mapping the json key to the markdown renderer changes that path from a dictionary-like object into a markdown string, so explicit --output json now returns a quoted markdown blob instead of the existing automation-facing object.
findings.test_gap: The visible CI only exercises the markdown snapshot path while the json contract coverage remains skipped, so this explicit json regression is not caught in this stack.
findings.title: The json renderer registry now routes explicit json requests to markdown

summary: The PR changes both the default CLI output and the explicit json export path, so it does not preserve the existing automation-facing JSON behavior described in the PR and repo contract.
